calibracion.title: Evaluation Calibration
calibracion.subtitle: Collaborative sessions to review and validate performance scores
calibracion.loadError: Could not load sessions. Please try reloading the page.
calibracion.createError: Error creating the session. Please try again.
calibracion.noSessionsHint: Create a session linked to a completed cycle to get started

guide.title: Guide: Evaluation Calibration
guide.whatIsDesc: A process where the leadership committee reviews and adjusts evaluation scores to ensure fairness and consistency across teams. Primarily used in 360° evaluations.
guide.whenUsedDesc: After all cycle evaluations are completed, before delivering results. It is a stage of the evaluation cycle activated in 360° evaluations.
guide.flowStep1: Create session linked to a closed cycle or calibration stage
guide.flowStep3: Review scores and make justified adjustments
guide.flowStep4: Adjustments greater than 1 point require written justification
guide.flowStep5: Complete the session — calibrated scores are applied to the Nine Box Matrix
guide.connCycles: Evaluation Cycles: calibration is a stage of the 360° cycle
guide.connTalent: Talent (Nine Box): calibrated results feed the talent matrix
guide.connReports: Reports: calibrated scores are reflected in analytics
guide.permAdmin: Administrator: Creates sessions, adjusts scores, closes calibration
guide.permManager: Team Manager: Can participate as reviewer in their department's sessions
guide.permEmployee: Employee: Does not have access to this feature

form.title: New calibration session
form.subtitle: Complete the details to create the session. Fields marked with * are required.
form.name: Name *
form.notesPlaceholder: Observations or context for the session...

calibracionDetalle.readOnly: 🔒 This session is completed and read-only. No further adjustments can be made.
calibracionDetalle.successMsg: Calibration session completed. Adjustments have been applied to the Nine Box Matrix.
calibracionDetalle.emptyState: No participants loaded in this session.
calibracionDetalle.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, position or department...
calibracionDetalle.noFilterResults: No results for the applied filters.

distribution.title: Score Distribution
distribution.deviatedAlert: ⚠ Deviated distribution — actual distribution does not match the expected curve.
distribution.howToReadDesc: Bars show how many employees scored in each range. The yellow line is the theoretical normal curve. If bars closely follow that line, the distribution is balanced.

objetivos.title: Objectives / OKR
objetivos.subtitle: Define measurable goals aligned with your organization's strategy
objetivos.treeNoData: No objectives to display in the tree
objetivos.treeHint: Objectives with an assigned 'parent objective' will appear here as a hierarchy

guide.title: Guide: Objectives
guide.desc: Objectives let you define measurable goals for each employee, aligned with the organization's strategy. They can be linked to evaluation cycles to measure completion in performance reviews.
guide.draftNote: Objectives you propose will remain in 'Draft' status until your manager approves them.
guide.typeOkrDesc: Define what you want to achieve (the objective) and how you'll know you achieved it (key results).
guide.typeKpiDesc: A number measured continuously to know if work is on track. Updated periodically and useful for detecting trends.
guide.typeSmartDesc: A specific, measurable, achievable, relevant and time-bound objective.
guide.flow: Flow: Create / Propose → Draft → Approve → In progress → Complete

form.title: Title
form.progressNote: You must provide a reason in the note — it will be recorded in the history.

talento.title: Talent Management
talento.subtitle: Nine Box Matrix and organizational talent segmentation
talento.generateSuccess: talent assessments generated successfully. Click a quadrant to view employees.
talento.editHint: Click any row to edit the employee's Potential, Readiness, and Flight Risk.
talento.selectCycle: Select a cycle to view talent segmentation.
talento.generateFirst: Generate an assessment first from the Nine Box tab.
talento.colPerformanceHint: Automatically calculated from the average of completed evaluations
talento.colPotentialHint: Score 0–10 entered manually by admin/manager. Click a row to edit.
talento.colReadinessHint: Promotion readiness — entered manually by admin/manager
talento.colFlightRiskHint: Employee turnover risk — entered manually by admin/manager
talento.segPotentialHint: Potential score assigned in the Nine Box tab
talento.segReadinessHint: Promotion readiness assigned in the Nine Box tab
talento.segFlightRiskHint: Flight risk assigned in the Nine Box tab
talento.matrixAxisNote: ↑ Y-Axis: Performance (based on evaluations). → X-Axis: Potential (assessed manually by manager/admin).

guide.title: How does Talent Management work?
guide.nineBoxDesc: Classifies employees in a 3×3 matrix crossing Performance (vertical axis) and Potential (horizontal axis). Admins can edit Potential, Readiness and Flight Risk directly in the table.
guide.segmentationDesc: Flat view of all classified employees, with search and pool filters. Useful for finding all 'Stars' without knowing which quadrant they are in.
guide.dataSourceTitle: Where does the data come from?
guide.dataPerformance: Performance: automatically calculated from the average of completed evaluations
guide.dataReadiness: Readiness: manually entered (Ready now / In 1 year / In 2 years / Not ready)

analytics.title: Cycle Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Statistical distribution, competency map and team performance

guide.title: Guide: Cycle Analytics
guide.desc: This page presents organizational statistics from evaluation results per cycle. Select a cycle to view metrics. Data comes from completed evaluations.
guide.chartBell: Score Distribution (Bell Curve): Histogram with superimposed normal curve. Shows whether distribution is balanced or biased.
guide.chartDept: Department Comparison: Average score per department.
guide.chartHeatmap: Heat map: Department × template section matrix. Green = high, yellow = medium, red = low.
guide.chartManagers: Manager ranking ordered by average score of their team members.
guide.connReports: Collaborator Reports: For individual analysis, go to the Reports section
guide.connCalibration: Calibration: If calibration was performed, adjusted scores are reflected here
guide.connTalent: Talent (Nine Box): Performance scores seen here feed the Nine Box performance axis
guide.permissions: Permissions: Only Administrators and Team Managers can access this page.

analytics.howToReadDesc: Bars show how many employees scored in each range. The yellow line is the theoretical normal curve. If bars closely follow that line, the distribution is balanced.
analytics.noDataCurve: Insufficient data for the distribution curve
analytics.minDataRequired: At least 5 evaluations are required to display the distribution
analytics.alertLeniency: ⚠️ More than 60% of evaluations fell in the high zone. Possible leniency bias — consider calibration.
analytics.alertHarshness: ⚠️ More than 60% of evaluations fell in the low zone. Possible severity bias or widespread performance issues.
analytics.alertCentral: ⚠️ High concentration in the middle range. Low differentiation — the scale may not be used effectively.
analytics.dispersionLow: Very low dispersion: reviewers tend to assign very similar scores.
analytics.dispersionHigh: High dispersion: there is a lot of variability between scores.
analytics.dispersionNormal: Normal dispersion: variability is healthy and allows good differentiation of performance levels.

reportes.title: Reports
reportes.subtitle: Performance analysis by cycle

guide.title: Guide: Reports
guide.desc: Reports consolidate evaluation cycle results. Select a cycle to view the executive summary with key metrics, department breakdown and export options.
guide.contentSummary: Executive summary: Total evaluations, overall average score, completion rate
guide.contentDept: Department breakdown: Average score, number of evaluations and comparative bar per area
guide.contentExport: Export: Download as CSV (for Excel) or PDF (for presentations)
guide.connAnalytics: Advanced Analytics: For more detailed charts use the Analytics page
guide.connCalibration: Calibration: Calibrated scores are automatically reflected in reports
guide.connIndividual: Individual performance: Click an employee to view their detailed history
guide.permissions: Permissions: Only Administrators and Team Managers can access reports.

reportes.excelNote: Excel includes 4 sheets: Summary, By Department, Evaluation Detail and Ranking by evaluatee.

postulantes.title: Applicant Evaluation
postulantes.subtitle: Evaluation processes for external and internal candidates

guide.title: Applicant Evaluation Guide
guide.whatIsDesc: The Applicant Evaluation module manages selection processes for internal and external candidates. Each process is linked to a specific position and uses the organization's competency model to evaluate candidates objectively and systematically. Multiple evaluators can independently rate each candidate, and the system automatically calculates average scores to facilitate comparison.
guide.flowStep1: The administrator creates an evaluation process specifying the position and department. Department managers are automatically assigned as evaluators, and guest evaluators from other areas can be added.
guide.flowStep2: Candidates are added to the process (new external applicants or existing internal employees).
guide.flowStep3: Each evaluator accesses the Scorecard tab and rates the candidate on each position competency (1-10 scale).
guide.flowStep4: The system automatically calculates the final score as the average of all evaluations received.
guide.flowStep5: The Comparative tab displays a matrix of all candidates vs competencies, with gap indicators against expected levels.
guide.flowStep6: The administrator updates each candidate's status based on the result: Approved, Rejected or Hired.
guide.rule1: Each candidate can only be added once per process (duplicate control).
guide.rule2: Evaluations use a 1 to 10 scale, with one score per competency per evaluator.
guide.rule3: The final score is automatically recalculated each time an evaluator submits or updates their evaluation.
guide.rule4: For internal candidates, the system displays additional talent pool data: performance, potential and Nine-Box position.
guide.rule5: Evaluated competencies correspond to those configured for the position in the Development module (Role Competencies).
guide.statusAppliedDesc: The candidate was added to the process but has not been evaluated yet.
guide.statusApprovedDesc: The candidate passed the evaluation process successfully.
guide.statusRejectedDesc: The candidate did not meet the process criteria.
guide.statusHiredDesc: The candidate was selected and hired for the position.
guide.connCompetencies: Role Competencies: competencies and expected levels come from the Development module.
guide.connTalent: Talent: for internal candidates, their performance evaluation, potential and Nine-Box position are displayed.
guide.connUsers: Users: internal candidates are linked to existing employees in the system.
guide.permAdmin: Administrator: creates processes, adds candidates, assigns evaluators, changes statuses and configures processes.
guide.permManager: Manager / Evaluator: evaluates candidates in assigned processes, views scorecards and comparatives.

dei.title: Diversity, Equity & Inclusion (DEI)
dei.subtitle: Organizational composition metrics and evaluation equity analysis
dei.loadError: Error loading DEI data. Verify you have administrator permissions.
dei.noAlerts: No bias alerts detected for this cycle.
dei.emptyState: No active users. Add employees to see DEI metrics.
dei.configDesc: Configure thresholds that determine when bias alerts are generated and the minimum people to show group metrics.
dei.actionPlaceholder: Describe the action to address the identified gap...

guide.title: Diversity, Equity & Inclusion Guide
guide.whatIsDesc: The DEI module monitors the organization's demographic composition and detects bias in performance evaluations. It analyzes gaps by gender, seniority, age, tenure, department and nationality, generating alerts when differences exceed configured thresholds.
guide.section1: Summary cards: total employees, genders, nationalities and age ranges represented.
guide.section2: Demographic distributions: composition bars for each dimension.
guide.section3: Data completeness: percentage of demographic fields filled by employees.
guide.section4: Equity analysis: select an evaluation cycle to see bias alerts and dimension gaps.
guide.section5: Corrective actions: register and track actions taken to address identified gaps.
guide.rule1: Groups with fewer than the configured minimum (default: 10) are hidden to protect privacy.
guide.rule2: Bias alerts are visible only to administrators and HRBP.
guide.rule3: Alert thresholds are configurable per organization.
guide.permAdmin: Administrator: views all metrics, configures thresholds, registers corrective actions.
guide.permManager: Manager: views demographic metrics and equity alerts for their organization.
